name: BA
definition: Batting average measures hits per at-bat.
formula: BA = H / AB
notes: Batting average ignores walks and power, so it works best alongside OBP or OPS.
historical_support: Computable from Lahman and Retrosheet batting totals.
live_support: Available from MLB Stats API hitting splits.

name: OPS
definition: OPS adds a hitter's on-base percentage and slugging percentage into one rate stat.
formula: OPS = OBP + SLG
notes: OPS is easy to compare across eras only when it is paired with context-adjusted stats like OPS+.
historical_support: Computable from Lahman batting totals and most game-level batting feeds.
live_support: Available from MLB Stats API season splits.

name: OPS+
definition: OPS+ adjusts a player's OPS for league scoring environment and park effects, with 100 set to league average.
formula: OPS+ is a park- and league-adjusted index statistic; exact implementation depends on league and park factors for the season.
notes: Because OPS+ is indexed to league average, 150 means 50 percent better than league average offense after adjustments.
historical_support: Not native in Lahman; best imported from Baseball-Reference-style tables or computed with park and league context.
live_support: Usually not available in official live APIs.

name: wOBA
definition: wOBA gives each offensive event a run-value weight instead of treating all times on base equally.
formula: wOBA = (unintentional BB weight * BB + HBP weight * HBP + 1B weight * 1B + 2B weight * 2B + 3B weight * 3B + HR weight * HR) / (AB + BB - IBB + SF + HBP)
notes: The weights change by run environment, so season-specific constants matter.
historical_support: Requires yearly linear-weight constants in addition to batting event totals.
live_support: Better sourced from pybaseball/FanGraphs-style exports than the MLB Stats API.

name: xwOBA
definition: xwOBA estimates what a hitter or contact profile should have produced based on Statcast quality-of-contact and strikeout or walk inputs.
formula: Provider model based on Statcast expected-outcome probabilities rather than a simple public closed-form equation.
notes: Exact implementation depends on provider modeling choices and Statcast-era data coverage.
historical_support: Needs Statcast event data and provider-style expected outcome modeling.
live_support: Usually sourced from Baseball Savant, Statcast exports, or advanced providers rather than the MLB Stats API.

name: xBA
definition: xBA estimates the batting average a hitter or contact profile deserved based on Statcast inputs.
formula: Provider model based on Statcast contact quality, strikeouts, and expected-outcome modeling.
notes: Exact implementation is provider-specific and tied to Statcast-era batted-ball data.
historical_support: Needs Statcast batted-ball data and expected-outcome modeling; not native to Lahman or Retrosheet.
live_support: Usually sourced from Baseball Savant, Statcast exports, or advanced providers rather than the MLB Stats API.

name: xSLG
definition: xSLG estimates expected slugging percentage from Statcast-based expected outcomes on contact.
formula: Provider model based on Statcast expected hit quality and expected bases on contact outcomes.
notes: Exact implementation is provider-specific and only available in the Statcast era.
historical_support: Needs Statcast event data and provider-style expected outcome modeling.
live_support: Usually sourced from Baseball Savant or advanced Statcast-facing providers.

name: wRC+
definition: wRC+ converts offensive production into runs created and then indexes it to league average, where 100 is average.
formula: wRC+ starts from wOBA and yearly wOBA scale constants, then adjusts for league and park context.
notes: The exact output depends on season constants and park adjustments, so it is not a single timeless arithmetic expression.
historical_support: Needs event totals plus seasonal constants and park context.
live_support: Usually sourced from advanced-stat providers rather than official live APIs.

name: WAR
definition: WAR estimates how many wins a player added over a replacement-level baseline.
formula: WAR is a composite framework combining batting, baserunning, fielding, positional adjustment, league adjustment, and replacement runs; implementations vary by provider.
notes: Baseball-Reference WAR and FanGraphs WAR are both valid but not interchangeable.
historical_support: Exact provider-specific WAR must be imported from the relevant source.
live_support: Live official APIs usually do not expose WAR directly.

name: FIP
definition: FIP estimates pitcher performance using strikeouts, walks, hit batters, and home runs while stripping out most fielding effects.
formula: FIP = ((13 * HR) + (3 * (BB + HBP)) - (2 * K)) / IP + constant
notes: The constant changes by run environment so league-average FIP lines up with league-average ERA.
historical_support: Computable from pitching event totals with a season-specific constant.
live_support: Often easier through pybaseball and advanced-stat providers than official live APIs.

name: xFIP
definition: xFIP replaces actual home runs with an expected home-run total based on fly balls and league HR/FB rate.
formula: xFIP = ((13 * expected HR) + (3 * (BB + HBP)) - (2 * K)) / IP + constant
notes: You need fly-ball counts and a league HR/FB baseline.
historical_support: Needs batted-ball context beyond core Lahman tables.
live_support: Usually sourced from advanced providers.

name: SIERA
definition: SIERA estimates pitcher skill by modeling how strikeouts, walks, and batted-ball tendencies interact.
formula: SIERA uses a regression-style formula with strikeout, walk, and batted-ball terms rather than a short closed-form equation.
notes: SIERA is best treated as a published model rather than a stat to recompute from memory.
historical_support: Needs expanded batted-ball detail and the published model coefficients.
live_support: Usually sourced from advanced providers.

name: ERA+
definition: ERA+ adjusts ERA for league and park context, with 100 equal to league average and higher numbers better.
formula: ERA+ is an indexed stat built from ERA, league ERA, and park factors.
notes: Because lower ERA is better, ERA+ is inverted so higher indexed values are better.
historical_support: Needs season-level park and league context in addition to earned runs and innings.
live_support: Usually not part of official live APIs.

name: WHIP
aliases: walks plus hits per inning pitched
definition: WHIP measures how many baserunners a pitcher allows via hits and walks per inning pitched.
formula: WHIP = (BB + H) / IP
notes: It ignores hit batters, errors, and quality of contact.
historical_support: Computable from Lahman or Retrosheet pitching data.
live_support: Easy to compute from live pitching splits.

name: BABIP
aliases: batting average on balls in play
definition: BABIP measures how often non-home-run balls in play fall for hits.
formula: BABIP = (H - HR) / (AB - K - HR + SF)
notes: Pitcher BABIP can say as much about defense and contact quality as it does about the pitcher.
historical_support: Computable when sacrifice flies are available.
live_support: Exposed in many live season stat feeds.

name: ISO
definition: ISO captures extra-base hit power independent of singles.
formula: ISO = SLG - AVG
notes: A hitter with strong slugging but modest batting average can still have an elite ISO.
historical_support: Computable from basic batting totals.
live_support: Computable from live batting splits.

name: K-BB%
definition: K-BB% measures the gap between strikeout rate and walk rate, often a fast way to summarize pitcher dominance and command.
formula: K-BB% = (K / BF) - (BB / BF)
notes: Batters faced is the clean denominator; plate appearances is a common practical substitute in some feeds.
historical_support: Computable when batters faced is available or inferable.
live_support: Often easiest through pybaseball or advanced exports.

name: CSW%
definition: CSW% measures the share of pitches that become either called strikes or swinging strikes.
formula: CSW% = (called strikes + swinging strikes) / total pitches
notes: CSW% is pitch-level and requires pitch outcome detail, so Lahman alone is not enough.
historical_support: Requires play-by-play or pitch-by-pitch data such as Statcast or detailed Retrosheet-style sources.
live_support: Best sourced through pybaseball Statcast workflows.

name: WPA
definition: WPA measures how each play changed a team's win expectancy and sums those changes by player.
formula: WPA is the sum of win expectancy deltas across the plays involving a player.
notes: WPA is context-heavy and answers 'what changed the game' better than 'who is the best player.'
historical_support: Requires play-by-play plus win expectancy tables.
live_support: Needs play-by-play context, not just box scores.

name: RE24
definition: RE24 measures a player's run impact using the change in run expectancy across the 24 base-out states.
formula: RE24 is the sum of run expectancy deltas plus runs scored during each event.
notes: It is context-based like WPA, but in runs instead of wins.
historical_support: Requires play-by-play data and a run expectancy matrix.
live_support: Needs event-level data, not just final totals.

name: REW
definition: REW converts run-expectancy value into wins, giving a wins-scale companion to RE24.
formula: REW is a provider-derived win conversion built from run-expectancy results rather than a basic box-score formula.
notes: It is best treated as a provider leaderboard stat rather than something to re-derive casually from season totals.
historical_support: Usually sourced from advanced providers with play-by-play context.
live_support: Usually sourced from FanGraphs-style leaderboards rather than official live APIs.

name: Clutch
definition: Clutch measures how a player performed in high-leverage spots relative to his overall baseline performance.
formula: Clutch compares context-dependent value like WPA to context-neutral performance, so it is a provider-style derived stat rather than a one-line box-score formula.
notes: Clutch is descriptive and volatile; it is not meant to replace broader talent metrics like WAR or wRC+.
historical_support: Requires play-by-play leverage and win-expectancy context.
live_support: Usually sourced from advanced providers such as FanGraphs.

name: LOB%
definition: LOB% estimates what share of baserunners a pitcher strands rather than allowing to score.
formula: LOB% is a provider stat tied to baserunner and scoring context; common public approximations exist, but provider implementations may vary slightly.
notes: Pitchers with unusually high or low strand rates often regress unless they also have unusual strikeout, contact, or sequencing skill.
historical_support: Usually sourced from advanced leaderboards or computed from detailed run-allowance context.
live_support: Available from FanGraphs-style pitching leaderboards.

name: tERA
definition: tERA estimates run prevention from strikeouts, walks, and the full shape of a pitcher's batted-ball profile.
formula: tERA is a provider-style run estimator built from detailed batted-ball outcome modeling rather than a short closed-form box-score equation.
notes: It is more granular than FIP because it cares about the type of contact a pitcher allows.
historical_support: Needs batted-ball-detail tables beyond core Lahman data.
live_support: Usually sourced from advanced providers such as FanGraphs.

name: Off
definition: Off is FanGraphs' offensive runs-above-average style value that rolls batting and baserunning offense into one runs-scale number.
formula: Off is provider-composed from batting and baserunning components, so the practical source is the published leaderboard value.
notes: It is useful for comparing total offensive impact on a runs scale rather than a rate scale.
historical_support: Usually sourced from FanGraphs leaderboards.
live_support: Usually sourced from FanGraphs leaderboards.

name: Bat
definition: Bat is FanGraphs' batting-runs component, isolating offensive production at the plate on a runs scale.
formula: Bat is a provider component derived from weighted offensive production rather than a single raw-stat formula.
notes: It excludes fielding and broader contextual framing, so it is best read as one component in a larger profile.
historical_support: Usually sourced from FanGraphs leaderboards.
live_support: Usually sourced from FanGraphs leaderboards.

name: Fld
definition: Fld is FanGraphs' fielding-runs component on a runs-above-average scale.
formula: Fld is sourced from provider defensive systems and component aggregation rather than a one-line public formula.
notes: It is related to, but not identical with, the standalone fielding systems behind the scenes.
historical_support: Usually sourced from provider-backed leaderboards.
live_support: Usually sourced from provider-backed leaderboards.

name: RAR
definition: RAR converts total player value into runs above replacement level.
formula: RAR is a replacement-level value framework on a runs scale, often serving as a bridge between runs and WAR.
notes: Because RAR is on a runs scale, it can be easier to connect to other runs-based components than WAR itself.
historical_support: Usually sourced from advanced leaderboards or provider exports.
live_support: Usually sourced from advanced leaderboards or provider exports.

name: RA9-WAR
definition: RA9-WAR is a wins-above-replacement flavor for pitchers built from runs allowed per nine innings instead of FIP-style components.
formula: RA9-WAR starts from actual runs allowed and then adjusts for context and replacement level, so provider details matter.
notes: It is often contrasted with FIP-based pitcher WAR because it gives more credit or blame for actual run prevention.
historical_support: Usually sourced from advanced provider leaderboards.
live_support: Usually sourced from advanced provider leaderboards.

name: RISP
definition: RISP is a situational split, not a standalone stat. It means the batting or pitching results that happened with a runner on second base, third base, or both.
formula: Use the normal formula inside only those plate appearances. Example: BA with RISP = H_with_RISP / AB_with_RISP.
notes: RISP performance is descriptive but highly sample-sensitive, so it usually needs context from opportunity counts and larger baseline stats.
historical_support: Computable from play-by-play or split tables that preserve base-state context, including Retrosheet plays.csv.
live_support: Available from public split providers and computable from detailed play-by-play feeds.

name: DRS
definition: DRS is a run-value defensive system built from multiple components such as range, throwing, double-play skill, bunts, and catcher framing depending on position.
formula: DRS is a proprietary composite system maintained by Sports Info Solutions; the broad component structure is public, but the full provider implementation is not fully transparent or reproducible from core public data alone.
notes: Exact single-game or season DRS leaderboards usually require provider data or exported tables from Baseball-Reference/StatMuse-style sources. If the dataset does not include DRS directly, the bot should say so instead of pretending it can derive the number exactly.
historical_support: Not computable exactly from Lahman or basic Retrosheet summary files.
live_support: Not exposed as an official live stat in the MLB Stats API.

name: rPM
definition: rPM is the FanGraphs/SIS label for the main DRS range-positioning component; on modern infield pages it reflects the PART-system version of that component.
formula: rPM is a provider-owned DRS component. FanGraphs notes that SIS moved infield DRS to the PART system in 2020 and surfaces that component as rPM, but the exact play-level model is not fully public.
notes: Older public descriptions often call this the Plus/Minus component. FanGraphs' March 17, 2020 update says the modern infield version uses starting-position information and the PART framework.
historical_support: Exact values require provider DRS data.
live_support: Not exposed as an official live stat in the MLB Stats API.

name: rARM
definition: rARM is the DRS outfield-arm component that credits or debits an outfielder for controlling runner advancement with his throws.
formula: rARM is a provider-owned DRS component based on runner advancement and throwing outcomes relative to expectation.
notes: This is not the same thing as a generic arm-strength measurement; it is a run-value result based on actual plays and advancement decisions.
historical_support: Exact values require provider DRS data.
live_support: Not exposed as an official live stat in the MLB Stats API.

name: rSB
definition: rSB is the DRS running-game component that measures value from controlling stolen bases and caught-stealing outcomes, primarily for catchers and pitchers.
formula: rSB is a provider-owned DRS component based on stolen-base attempts, caught stealings, and related run-value context.
notes: FanGraphs uses rSB as part of the DRS component family, but the exact implementation remains provider-sourced.
historical_support: Exact values require provider DRS data.
live_support: Not exposed as an official live stat in the MLB Stats API.

name: rGDP
definition: rGDP is the DRS component that credits infielders for turning or helping create double plays better or worse than average.
formula: rGDP is a provider-owned DRS component based on double-play opportunities and outcomes relative to expectation.
notes: This is a component of DRS, not a standalone all-purpose fielding stat.
historical_support: Exact values require provider DRS data.
live_support: Not exposed as an official live stat in the MLB Stats API.

name: rBU
definition: rBU is the DRS bunt-defense component that measures how well a fielder handles bunt chances relative to average.
formula: rBU is a provider-owned DRS component based on bunt opportunities and outcomes relative to expectation.
notes: Bunt value can matter most for corner infielders, catchers, and pitchers, but exact provider credit is source-specific.
historical_support: Exact values require provider DRS data.
live_support: Not exposed as an official live stat in the MLB Stats API.

name: rHR
definition: rHR is the DRS home-run-robbing component, usually discussed when an outfielder takes away a would-be home run at the wall.
formula: rHR is a provider-owned DRS component and should be treated as sourced data rather than a public formula.
notes: FanGraphs and older DRS explanations discuss home-run robbing as part of the DRS component family, but the synced public SIS endpoint in this project does not expose rHR as a separate column.
historical_support: Needs provider component data beyond the synced public SIS leaderboard feed.
live_support: Not exposed as an official live stat in the MLB Stats API.

name: Def
definition: Def is FanGraphs' overall defensive value stat for position players, combining fielding runs with positional adjustment into one runs-based number.
formula: Def = fielding runs above average + positional adjustment. On FanGraphs this is a provider-specific stat family rather than a single universal implementation.
notes: Def is not the same thing as DRS. It is a broader FanGraphs defensive value output that incorporates positional difficulty on top of the underlying fielding metric.
historical_support: Best sourced directly from FanGraphs-style exports or other provider tables.
live_support: Usually not available in official live APIs.

name: OAA
definition: OAA is a Statcast fielding measure that compares the probability of making a play to whether the fielder actually made it.
formula: OAA is built from play-level catch or fielding probabilities from tracked ball and fielder movement data.
notes: OAA is a better public proxy than DRS when you need tracked-play fielding context from Statcast.
historical_support: Only available in the Statcast era and usually through Baseball Savant-style data.
live_support: Can be approximated through Statcast/pybaseball workflows, not standard box-score APIs.

name: UZR
definition: UZR converts fielding plays into run value using zone-based expectations.
formula: UZR is a provider-specific zone-based defensive model rather than a simple public box-score formula.
notes: Like DRS, UZR should be treated as sourced data, not something to reverse-engineer from Lahman alone.
historical_support: Needs provider defensive data.
live_support: Usually not available in official live APIs.

name: OBP
definition: OBP measures how often a hitter reaches base through hits, walks, or hit by pitches.
formula: OBP = (H + BB + HBP) / (AB + BB + HBP + SF)
notes: Intentional walks are usually included in standard OBP.
historical_support: Computable from batting event totals.
live_support: Available from MLB Stats API season splits.

name: SLG
definition: SLG measures total bases per at-bat.
formula: SLG = total bases / AB
notes: SLG rewards extra-base hits but ignores walks.
historical_support: Computable from batting totals.
live_support: Available from MLB Stats API season splits.
